summary: Deduplicate the byte-identical ShutDown()/Init() lifecycle boilerplate shared by KVClient and ObjectClient by extracting two free helpers (ClientShutDown, ClientInit) into the datasystem::object_cache namespace, declared in object_client_impl.h and defined in object_client_impl.cpp, then rewriting the four wrapper methods as one-line delegations. Both wrappers already hold std::shared_ptr<object_cache::ObjectClientImpl> impl_ and depend on the object_client_impl target, so no build-system changes are needed and behaviour is preserved byte-for-byte (including that ShutDown null-checks impl_ while Init does not).

ledger.what: Use two free helper functions in datasystem::object_cache rather than a shared base class.
ledger.why: A base class would alter the hierarchy of two public-API types in include/datasystem/* (larger blast radius, header/ABI churn); both wrappers already share the same std::shared_ptr<ObjectClientImpl> impl_ member and already depend on the object_client_impl target, so free functions are the minimal, surgical fix.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose free functions ClientShutDown/ClientInit taking const std::shared_ptr<ObjectClientImpl>&.

blast_radius.why: Touches 2 call sites plus 1 header declaration and 1 cpp definition; no public API signatures change.

reversibility.why: Can migrate to a base class later without changing the wrappers' observable behaviour.

ledger.revisit_if: A third client wrapper appears or the shared lifecycle grows shared state that wants a common owner.

ledger.what: Both KVClient::impl_ and ObjectClient::impl_ are std::shared_ptr<object_cache::ObjectClientImpl>, and ObjectClientImpl::ShutDown/Init/CompleteHandler are public.
ledger.why: The helper must accept a shared_ptr<ObjectClientImpl> and invoke these methods from outside the class.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified against include/datasystem/kv_client.h:415 and include/datasystem/object_client.h:219 (both std::shared_ptr<object_cache::ObjectClientImpl> impl_), and object_client_impl.h:94 (public:), :107 (ShutDown), :125 (Init), :447 (CompleteHandler, above private: at :686).

blast_radius.why: A fact-check underpinning the helper signature; wrong only if the impl type or visibility changes.

reversibility.why: Signature can be adjusted if the impl type changes before implementation.

ledger.revisit_if: The impl pointer type or the visibility of ShutDown/Init/CompleteHandler changes.

ledger.what: ClientShutDown keeps the impl null-check; ClientInit does NOT null-check and dereferences impl directly.
ledger.why: Preserve byte-for-byte current behaviour: today's ShutDown guards impl_ and returns Status::OK() when null, while today's Init dereferences impl_->Init unconditionally. Adding a guard to Init would be an out-of-scope behavioural change.
ledger.what_i_did: Mirrored the exact current asymmetry in the two helpers.

blast_radius.why: Behaviour-preserving; affects only the null-impl edge case of Init, which is unchanged from today.

reversibility.why: A null-check can be added to ClientInit later as a separate, explicitly-scoped change.

ledger.revisit_if: A decision is made to harden Init against a null impl_.

ledger.what: Declare helpers in object_client_impl.h and define them in object_client_impl.cpp; add no new files and no build-file edits.
ledger.why: object_cache/BUILD.bazel globs *.cpp into the object_client target, client/kv_cache/BUILD.bazel already deps object_client_impl, and both wrappers already #include object_client_impl.h, so the helpers are reachable from both with zero build wiring.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose the existing header/cpp as the home for the helpers.

blast_radius.why: No translation units or build targets added; only two existing files gain content.

reversibility.why: Helpers can be relocated to a new file with a small BUILD edit if ever needed.

ledger.revisit_if: The object_cache target stops globbing *.cpp or the header is split.

ledger.what: KVClient::InitEmbedded() (kv_client.cpp:86-95) shares a similar TraceGuard + InitKvMetrics + CompleteHandler shape but is left untouched.
ledger.why: It is KVClient-only (no duplicated copy to remove) and the issue explicitly scopes the work to ShutDown()/Init(); folding it in would broaden the change beyond the request.
ledger.what_i_did: Left InitEmbedded out of scope and documented it as deferred.

blast_radius.why: Deferring it changes nothing; it remains as-is.

reversibility.why: Can be folded into a shared helper later if a second InitEmbedded-shaped path appears.

ledger.revisit_if: A second InitEmbedded-shaped path appears, creating real duplication.

ledger.what: Do not add a new heavyweight system test; rely on existing ST coverage that drives client Init()/ShutDown().
ledger.why: No lightweight unit-test harness exists for the KVClient/ObjectClient wrappers (no tests/ut/** references them) and exercising the helpers requires a running worker; the change is a pure behaviour-preserving extraction, so existing STs (embedded_client, client/stream_cache, worker/object_cache) are the correct gate.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose to gate on build success plus existing ST behaviour rather than author a new ST.

blast_radius.why: Verification-strategy choice; the refactor itself is behaviour-preserving.

reversibility.why: A dedicated regression or fake-impl unit test can be added later.

ledger.revisit_if: Review requires a dedicated regression test or a lightweight fake-impl unit harness becomes available.
